= OAuth Flow Design (Tier 2)

Unlocks google_calendar, google_analytics, youtube_analytics, withings.
The OAuth *client* logic ships in the plugins (Signet); what's missing is
the web plumbing Core provides. Implementation waits until the
pasted-token work lands (Gemfile is in flight there).

== Constraints discovered in the vendored code

. Plugins build their redirect URI themselves, with a *fixed path*:
  `"#{Rails.application.credentials.base_url}/plugin_settings/<name>/redirect"`.
  Terminus must therefore serve that exact path, and `credentials.base_url`
  must return the Terminus public URL — while asset URLs stay pinned to
  usetrmnl.com. Two ENV vars: `PLUGINS_BASE_URI` (OAuth host, defaults to
  API_URI) and the existing `PLUGINS_ASSET_URI`.
. Class methods provide the flow pieces: `Plugins::<X>.redirect_url`
  (authorization URI) and `.fetch_access_token(code)` (token exchange).
. Tokens live in `plugin_settings.encrypted_settings[<keyname>]` and are
  *written back* on refresh via `plugin_settings.update(encrypted_settings:)`.
  Our Setting shim currently no-ops `update` — refreshed tokens would be
  lost and re-refreshed every render after expiry.

== Design

. *Routes* (web slice):
  - `GET /extensions/:id/oauth` — looks up the extension's plugin class,
    redirects to `klass.redirect_url`. Stores extension id in the session
    (the provider round-trip must recover which extension initiated).
  - `GET /plugin_settings/:plugin/redirect` — the fixed callback path.
    Exchanges `params[:code]` via `klass.fetch_access_token`, writes the
    token hash into the initiating extension's `data["secrets"][<keyname>]`,
    redirects back to the extension edit page.
. *Storage*: `extension.data["secrets"]` (jsonb). Plaintext at rest — same
  trust model as extension headers/API keys already stored there. A future
  upgrade can move to a pgcrypto column.
. *Setting shim*: accept an `on_update:` callback in `Plugins.render`;
  `Setting#update(encrypted_settings:)` invokes it so the native renderer
  can persist refreshed tokens back onto the extension row.
. *Gems*: `signet`, `google-apis-calendar_v3`, `google-apis-analyticsdata_v1beta`,
  the YouTube Analytics API gem; withings needs none but requires
  reimplementing Core's `OauthService::Withings` (standard token exchange —
  follow the plugin's call sites).
. *ENV app credentials*: `PLUGINS_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`/`_SECRET`,
  `PLUGINS_TODOIST_CLIENT_ID`/`_SECRET` (already resolved by the
  credentials stub) — users supply their own Google Cloud project creds,
  documented in STATUS.adoc.

== Open questions for upstream

. Is `data["secrets"]` acceptable, or should this wait for an encrypted
  column type in Terminus?
. Session-vs-state-param for recovering the initiating extension
  (state param is the OAuth-correct answer; Signet supports `state`).
